title: Cross-Encoder, Late-Interaction, Diversity, and Reranking · Program Brief: Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape
summary: A cross-encoder scores the whole corpus online, reranks only five dense candidates, ignores latency and diversity, and is declared better from one question. Predict the stakeholder decision, corpus and query identity, relevance assumption, failure cost, and evidence that would falsify the proposed RAG claim before opening the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the retrieve rerank shape increment in budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, expl…
objectives: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
Decompose bounded multi-part or multi-hop questions, preserve dependencies, and ask for clarification when ambiguity exceeds policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-worked-rag-reranking-4
# Competency: rag-worked-rag-reranking-4.
# Case 1655: an incident-response knowledge search.
# Operating constraint: measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score.
# Changed case: double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope.
def worked_rag_reranking_4(candidate_count: int = 40, rerank_count: int = 12, accepted_count: int = 5, cross_encoder_ms: float = 36.0, late_interaction: bool = False, evaluated: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "query4dekrow",
        "tenant1655",
        "indexworked41655",
        "prompt4dekrow1655",
        "evaluation1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if candidate_count <= 0 or rerank_count <= 0:
        return False, 0, "candidate-budget"
    if rerank_count > candidate_count or accepted_count > rerank_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "rerank-shape"
    if cross_encoder_ms <= 0.0:
        return False, accepted_count, "latency-evidence"
    if late_interaction and rerank_count < accepted_count:
        return False, accepted_count, "late-interaction-state"
    if not evaluated:
        return False, accepted_count, "quality-gate"
    assert 0 < accepted_count <= rerank_count
    return True, accepted_count, "reranked-context"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python RAG decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected query or corpus case, then state which native model, vector-store, provider, network, load, privacy, security, recovery, or deployment claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape model · Theory Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory RAG engineering team handles case 1809 during an enterprise retrieval migration canary. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget bi-encoder candidates, cross-encoder or late-interaction reranking, quality, and latency; record the failed stage, causal trace, repair, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.

Evidence target: RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape model · Theory Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RAG and Python Defend the Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape model · Theory Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape, keep Rag Retrieve Rerank Shape connected to executable Python retrieval, grounding, evaluation, and evidence decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
